data.title: Bank of america outlines 6-7% NII growth for 2025 with $15.5B Q4 target
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bank of America (BAC) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Brian Moynihan, CEO, highlighted strong Q1 2025 results with $7.4 billion in net income and $0.90 in EPS. He noted a 6% revenue growth year-over-year, driven by net interest income and wealth management performance. Moynihan emphasized deposit growth, which reached nearly $2 trillion, marking the seventh consecutive quarter of expansion.
  • Moynihan pointed out the addition of 7,200 net new households within wealth management and net AUM flows of $24 billion for the quarter. The consumer business achieved its 25th consecutive quarter of net new checking account growth, with $22 billion in annual consumer investment flows.
  • Alastair Borthwick, CFO, reported $27.5 billion in Q1 revenue, reflecting a 6% increase year-over-year. He confirmed 3% net interest income growth, strong performance in sales and trading, and stable provision expenses. Borthwick highlighted a $14.6 billion NII on a fully taxable equivalent basis.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated NII guidance for Q4 2025, targeting an exit rate of $15.5 billion to $15.7 billion. Key assumptions include modest loan and deposit growth, fixed rate asset repricing, and expected interest rate cuts later in the year.
  • Asset repricing is expected to contribute significant benefits, with $8 billion to $9 billion of securities paydowns and $4 billion to $5 billion in mortgage loans repricing each quarter, gaining over 200 basis points.
  • Full-year 2025 NII growth is projected at 6% to 7%.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 EPS of $0.90 and net income of $7.4 billion reflected an 18% year-over-year EPS increase. Revenue grew to $27.5 billion, up 6% from Q1 2024.
  • Sales and trading revenue reached $5.6 billion, marking a 9% year-over-year increase, driven by equities and fixed income.
  • Non-interest expense was $17.8 billion, influenced by seasonal payroll taxes and litigation costs.
  • The CET1 ratio stood at 11.8%, supported by $6.5 billion capital return to shareholders during the quarter.

Q&A

  • Steven Chubak, Wolfe Research: Questioned the sustainability of $4.5 billion in quarterly buybacks. CFO Alastair Borthwick confirmed flexibility in capital deployment and highlighted ongoing RWA growth.
  • John McDonald, Truist Securities: Asked about credit reserve assumptions. Borthwick stated reserves are modeled around a 6% unemployment rate scenario, reflecting conservative provisioning.
  • Erika Najarian, UBS: Sought clarification on NIM projections. Management reaffirmed a 2.3% long-term target, contingent on stable rate environments and asset repricing.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed cautious optimism, focusing on NII growth sustainability and credit quality. Questions reflected concerns about macroeconomic uncertainties and regulatory developments.
  • Management maintained confidence during the call, emphasizing strong underlying business fundamentals and robust credit positioning. Moynihan noted, "We are well-reserved for our portfolio and risk profile."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, with increased emphasis on growth in deposits and wealth management.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • NII grew 3% from Q4 2024, exceeding prior guidance ranges. Deposit growth continued for the seventh consecutive quarter.
  • Provision expenses remained stable at $1.5 billion, while credit quality metrics, including net charge-offs, showed improvement.
  • Management's confidence in achieving $15.5 billion to $15.7 billion NII by Q4 2025 was reiterated, despite macroeconomic uncertainties.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted potential economic challenges, including market volatility and policy uncertainties. Mitigation strategies include maintaining high-quality credit portfolios and leveraging strong liquidity positions.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the impact of potential interest rate cuts on NII growth and the sustainability of current loan and deposit trends.

Final Takeaway

Bank of America delivered a strong start to 2025 with robust growth in NII and deposits. Management remains confident in achieving full-year NII growth of 6% to 7%, targeting $15.5 billion to $15.7 billion by Q4. Continued investment in wealth management and consumer banking underpins their growth trajectory, while conservative credit provisioning positions the bank well for potential macroeconomic headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: UAW members at General Dynamics Electric Boat approve strike authorization
data.description:
Unionized workers at General Dynamics Electric Boat (NYSE:GD), a division of the defense contractor, voted to authorize a strike, the United Auto Workers announced Tuesday. The move escalates a labor standoff following the expiration of their contract earlier this month.

The dispute affects more than 2,400 marine design specialists responsible for drafting submarine plans at Electric Boat, a key unit in General Dynamics’ (NYSE:GD) marine systems segment that builds nuclear-powered submarines for the U.S. Navy.

The workers’ contract expired on April 4, and negotiations have failed to produce an agreement on key demands.

Union members are pushing for cost-of-living adjustments to offset rising inflation, expanded access to affordable healthcare and the reinstatement of pension benefits.

According to the UAW, General Dynamics (GD) has proposed steep hikes in weekly health insurance premiums ranging from 52% to 161%, which the union argues would significantly burden employees.

Electric Boat's role in the Navy’s submarine program includes the production of Virginia-class submarines. The division has struggled with production delays linked to labor shortages, design complications and supply chain disruptions stemming from the pandemic. 

Despite receiving major Pentagon contracts, the company has fallen behind schedule, with union leaders blaming lagging compensation for difficulties in attracting and retaining skilled workers.

“Inferior pay and benefits at Electric Boat are contributing to staffing shortages and project delays,” the UAW said in a statement.

General Dynamics (GD) Chief Executive Phebe Novakovic in January acknowledged ongoing margin pressures in the marine systems business. She  cites additional setbacks and quality issues within the submarine supply chain that continue to drive up costs.

While the strike authorization does not automatically trigger a walkout, it gives the union the power to call one if negotiations fail to make progress.

Talks are expected to continue as both sides seek a resolution to avoid disruptions to critical defense production.

data.title: Short sellers reduce bets against S&P500 information technology stocks in March
data.description:
Short sellers reduced their bets against the information technology sector in March. The sector which holds the highest weightage on the S&P had an average short interest of 1.74% in March, down from 1.80% at the end of February. 

The S&P 500’s information technology sector (NYSEARCA:XLK) has declined more than 13% so far this year, compared to a more than 8% decline in the broader benchmark index. 

Super Micro Computer (SMCI) remained the most shorted stock in the sector in March, while Microsoft (MSFT) took Teledyne Technologies (TDY)’s spot as the least shorted stock. 

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

Super Micro Computer (SMCI), Enphase Energy (ENPH), and Western Digital (WDC) were the top three most shorted stocks in March, just like the previous month. Short interest in SMCI rose to 22.84% in March, from 21.34% at the end of February. 

While, Enphase Energy (ENPH) and Western Digital (WDC) had a short interest of 14.29%, and 9.34% respectively. 

Microsoft (MSFT) was the least shorted stock in the sector last month, with a short interest of 0.68%, preceded by Apple (AAPL) and Keysight Technologies (KEYS), with short interests of 0.75% and 0.94% respectively. 

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

Electronic Manufacturing Services was the most shorted industry within the information technology sector, with 3.36% short interest in March, up from 2.74% in February. 

IT Services was the second most shorted industry with 2.31% short interest as of March 31, compared to 2.29% at the end of February. 

Electronic Equipment, Instruments & Components was the least shorted industry in the sector this month as well, with a short interest of 1.39%.

data.title: PNC Financial Services updates 2025 guidance amid strong Q1 results and strategic expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The PNC Financial Services Group, Inc. (PNC) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Bill Demchak highlighted that PNC achieved strong results in Q1 2025, reporting net income of $1.5 billion or $3.51 per share. Key drivers included a 3% growth in spot commercial and industrial (C&I) loans and effective expense management leading to net interest margin expansion. He noted the bank's continued focus on organic growth, adding new customers, and deepening relationships across its national network.

  • Demchak introduced Mark Wiedman as the new President, emphasizing his extensive experience in financial services and noting that Wiedman will focus on overseeing the bank's business operations. Demchak stated that "Mark brings deep experience in financial services that will complement the strength of our existing team."

  • CFO Rob Reilly reported tangible book value per common share increased to $100.40, a 5% sequential increase. He also detailed that the bank returned approximately $800 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases during the first quarter.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated full-year 2025 guidance, projecting stable average loans with spot loan growth of 2% to 3%, net interest income growth of 6% to 7%, and noninterest income growth of approximately 5%, resulting in total revenue growth of around 6%. Noninterest expenses are expected to rise by approximately 1%, with an effective tax rate of approximately 19%.

  • For Q2 2025, PNC expects average loans to increase by approximately 1%, net interest income to grow between 1% and 2%, and fee income to rise between 1% and 3%. The bank forecasts total revenue growth of 1% to 3% and stable noninterest expenses.

  • CEO Demchak commented on the uncertainty caused by proposed tariffs but maintained confidence in the resilience of PNC's business model and its ability to achieve financial targets.

Financial Results

  • Compared to Q4 2024, PNC reported a 2% decline in total revenue to $5.5 billion, attributed to seasonal factors and fewer business days in the quarter. However, net interest margin increased by 3 basis points to 2.78%.

  • Fee income totaled $1.8 billion, decreasing by 2% sequentially. Capital markets and advisory fees fell by 12%, while asset management and brokerage income increased by 5%, driven by higher client activity and positive flows.

  • Credit quality remained stable, with nonperforming loans at $2.3 billion and a net charge-off ratio of 26 basis points. The allowance for credit losses totaled $5.2 billion, representing 1.64% of total loans.

Q&A

  • John Pancari, Evercore: Asked about the drivers of C&I loan growth, specifically whether precautionary line draws were impacting balances. CFO Rob Reilly clarified that loan growth was broad-based across categories, with utilization rates gradually normalizing.

  • Bill Carcache, Wolfe Research: Inquired about expense leverage in case of a mild recession. CFO Reilly stated that PNC would manage expenses prudently while maintaining planned investments.

  • Betsy Graseck, Morgan Stanley: Questioned the strategic role of new President Mark Wiedman. CEO Demchak emphasized Wiedman’s expertise in markets and leadership, while clarifying that his appointment would not alter PNC’s strategic direction.

  • Scott Siefers, Piper Sandler: Sought confirmation on net interest margin trajectory. CFO Reilly affirmed that PNC aims to approach a 2.90% to 3% NIM by Q4 2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on PNC's ability to deliver growth amid economic uncertainties. Questions around loan growth and fee income highlighted skepticism regarding broader industry trends.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing the bank's strong balance sheet and robust credit quality. Phrases like "we remain well-positioned" and "we are confident" signaled assurance in achieving financial targets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, with management reiterating its 2025 revenue and earnings projections. However, Q1 results exhibited slight revenue declines due to seasonality.

  • Strategic focus shifted towards mitigating potential tariff impacts and continuing organic growth through customer acquisition and market expansion.

  • Analyst scrutiny intensified on loan growth and fee income volatility, reflecting concerns over broader macroeconomic challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified potential risks from proposed tariffs, which could pressure noninterest income and client activity. The bank is actively monitoring the situation and adjusting its models to account for potential downside scenarios.

  • Analysts raised concerns regarding loan growth sustainability and fee income stability, particularly in capital markets and advisory services.

Final Takeaway

PNC delivered a solid Q1 2025 performance, supported by disciplined expense management and a diversified business model. Key strategic initiatives, including market expansion and leadership reinforcement with the appointment of Mark Wiedman, position the bank to navigate macroeconomic uncertainties effectively. Management reaffirmed its full-year growth targets, maintaining confidence in achieving positive operating leverage and stable credit quality.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Benchmark sees MercadoLibre as a dominant force in LatAm, starts bullishly
data.description:
Analysts at Benchmark initiated coverage of Latin American e-commerce giant MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) with an investment rating of "Buy" on Tuesday.

The research firm said the company is a dominant regional leader in the global e-commerce setting, leveraging underpenetrated markets in LatAm that are primed for significant growth in both online retail and fintech.

"As the undisputed leader in the region, accounting for one quarter of online retail market share in Latam, MELI is well-positioned to capitalize on this multiyear growth story in ecommerce," Oppenheimer said in their April 15 note.

They believe the company offers investors a robust, sustainable, long-term growth outlook and has an extensive understanding of its growing buyer base and substantial potential to expand in the underserved fintech market through effective cross-selling and up-selling.

MELI has a PT of $2,500, implying an upside of 22.7%. Stock is up 22% YTD while the S&P 500 is down 8.1%.

data.title: Lucid Group plans to enter the midsize electric SUV market in 2026
data.description:
Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) confirmed on Tuesday that it is on track to enter the midsize electric SUV market in 2026. The electric vehicle maker could challenge the dominance of the Tesla (TSLA) Model Y if it can bring its new model to the market at a price point of around $50,000. Other models that Lucid's (NASDAQ:LCID) new SUV could impact include the Ford (F) Mustang Mach-E, Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF) Ioniq 5 and the upcoming Rivian (RIVN) R2.

The new model will join the Lucid Air sedan and Lucid Gravity SUV in the company's lineup. Lucid Group (LCID) also plans to launch the less-expensive Touring variant of the Gravity SUV later this year, starting at $79,900. The more affordable version of the Gravity SUV, will feature slightly less power and range than the Grand Touring version, but similar tech and space.

In terms of tariff disruption, Lucid Group (LCID) thinks it can mitigate the negative impact through prior agreements with battery cell and graphite suppliers. "We have those agreements already. The plants are being built right now, so it's not something that we can switch on today, but it's in the near future," highlighted interim CEO Marc Winterhoff.

Shares of Lucid Group (LCID) are down 18.1% on a year-to-date, vs. -36.8% for the Dow Jones U.S. Auto Manufacturers Index (DJUSAU).

data.title: Canada pushes back on report Honda moving production south
data.description:
In response to a Nikkei report claiming Honda Motor Co (NYSE:HMC) is moving production out of Canada to avert U.S. tariffs, the country’s Federal Industry Minister Anita Anand and Ontario Premier Doug Ford both insist the reports are false.

In Tuesday’s Nikkei Asia, Honda (NYSE:HMC) is reportedly working on plans to move production from Mexico and Canada into the U.S., increasing production of U.S.-made vehicles by as much as 30% over the next two to three years, enabling the Japanese automaker to meet 90% of sales in the country with domestically produced cars.

“We have agreements in place with the auto manufacturers. That’s our Strategic Investment Fund, and we will continue to ensure that we come forward to maintain a healthy and vibrant auto manufacturing sector,” Anand said in an interview on CTV earlier this month.

Anand also referenced a “remission framework” that would offer auto manufacturers incentives to remain in the country, a plan that was unveiled on Tuesday by Canadian finance minister Francois-Philippe Champagne.

While Honda said it plans to increase production in the U.S., this will not come at the expense of Canadian jobs, according to Ontario Premier Ford.

Honda (NYSE:HMC) currently employs 4,200 Canadians at its Alliston, Ontario plant, which makes the Honda Civic and Honda CR-V.

Honda (HMC) shares are up for a third straight day.

data.title: Evolution Mining approves A$430M capex for Cowal mine expansion
data.description:
Evolution Mining (OTCPK:CAHPF) +2.3% in Tuesday's trading after approving a A$430M expansion of its flagship Cowal gold operation in Australia's New South Wales, extending the open pit mine’s life to 2042 and unlocking an additional 2M oz of production.

Evolution's (OTCPK:CAHPF) board sanctioned the seven-year Cowal expansion project after receiving regulatory approvals from Australia's federal government and the NSW Department of Planning, Housing and Infrastructure, and said the project would generate an internal rate of return of 71% at current spot gold prices.

The company said the project will extend open pit operations by a decade through continued mining at the E42 pit and development of three new satellite pits located around the main deposit, and support the growth of Cowal's underground mine, which is expected to ramp up to 2.4M metric tons/year by FY 2026 and contribute ~30% of mill feed and 50% of total gold output.

Cowal has been a key part of Evolution's (OTCPK:CAHPF) portfolio since its acquisition in 2015, generating more than A$1.62B in net mine cash flow, including A$479M in the first nine months of the current financial year, and it expects the operation will remain a significant cash contributor throughout the open pit expansion project execution phase.

data.title: Blend Labs expands alliance with Alloy to beef up fraud prevention for banks
data.description:
Blend Labs (NYSE:BLND) expanded its partnership with Alloy to make it "even easier and more cost-effective for lenders to access top-tier risk and fraud prevention tools," Blend Co-Founder and CEO Nima Ghamsari said in a Tuesday statement.

As part of the new pact, Blend's (NYSE:BLND) customers can now more smoothly and cost-effectively access Alloy's identity and fraud prevention capabilities into their consumer banking solutions.

Specifically, the partnership enables financial institutions to meet know-your-customer ("KYC") requirements and screen for fraud by leveraging a range of third-party data sources.

A variety of financial providers use Blend's (BLND) origination platform for digital banking solutions, from large banks to fintechs to credit unions to community and independent mortgage lenders.

BLND shares gained 1.8% in Tuesday afternoon trading.

data.title: Johnson & johnson outlines $92B 2025 sales guidance amid innovative medicine and medtech growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Joaquin Duato highlighted strong operational sales growth of 4.2% with contributions from both Innovative Medicine and MedTech segments. He emphasized the company's resilience despite the loss of exclusivity for STELARA, attributing performance to their diversified healthcare model.
  • In Innovative Medicine, DARZALEX achieved over 20% growth, with approvals expanding indications in Europe. Other key brands, such as TREMFYA, saw accelerated growth due to new launches in inflammatory bowel disease.
  • MedTech reported 4.1% operational sales growth, driven by cardiovascular units Abiomed and Shockwave. Notable milestones include updates to guidelines for the Impella heart pump and advancements in robotic surgery with new clinical trials for OTTAVA.
  • CFO Joseph Wolk confirmed adjusted net earnings of $6.7 billion and EPS of $2.77 for the quarter. He noted the reversal of a $7 billion talc-related reserve and highlighted the company’s ongoing capital allocation strategy, including a 4.8% dividend increase.
  • EVP John Reed discussed pipeline progress, underscoring the potential of assets like RYBREVANT, TREMFYA, and icotrokinra, along with the anticipated approval of CAPLYTA for major depressive disorder later in the year.

Outlook

  • Full-year 2025 operational sales growth is projected at 3.3%-4.3%, with a midpoint of $92 billion. This includes contributions from the Intra-Cellular acquisition.
  • Adjusted EPS guidance remains $10.50-$10.70, accounting for dilution from the Intra-Cellular acquisition.
  • Management anticipates higher growth in the second half of 2025, supported by newly launched products and pipeline advancements.
  • Biosimilar competition for STELARA is expected to intensify through the year, with incremental revenue contributions from TREMFYA and other immunology assets mitigating the impact.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 worldwide sales reached $21.9 billion, with 5.9% growth in the US.
  • Innovative Medicine delivered $13.9 billion in sales, reflecting 4.2% growth despite an 810 basis point headwind from STELARA.
  • MedTech achieved $8 billion in sales, with growth supported by cardiovascular products and surgical vision innovations.
  • Adjusted net earnings were $6.7 billion, representing a 1.9% increase over Q1 2024.
  • Gross margin was impacted by biosimilar competition and product mix changes, including the decline of STELARA.

Q&A

  • Lawrence Biegelsen, Wells Fargo, inquired about tariff impacts. CFO Wolk explained that MedTech tariffs accounted for a significant portion of the $400 million guidance impact but noted ongoing mitigation strategies.
  • Chris Schott, JPMorgan, asked about gross margin declines. Wolk cited STELARA's higher margins and Part D redesign as primary drivers, with management expecting partial margin recovery in subsequent quarters.
  • Asad Haider, Goldman Sachs, questioned STELARA's erosion trajectory. EVP Jennifer Taubert reaffirmed guidance using HUMIRA’s biosimilar erosion pattern as a model.
  • Danielle Antalffy, UBS, explored recession resilience. Management expressed confidence in healthcare demand stability, noting robust procedures and pharmaceutical utilization.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a cautiously optimistic tone, focusing on tariff impacts, margin recovery, and STELARA's erosion trajectory.
  • Management maintained a confident stance during prepared remarks, emphasizing portfolio resilience and innovation. In Q&A, responses were measured, with clarity on challenges and mitigation strategies.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, reinforcing confidence in guidance, while analysts displayed slightly increased scrutiny regarding macroeconomic and competitive pressures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Sales growth moderated from Q4 2024, reflecting STELARA’s intensifying biosimilar competition.
  • Guidance language remained firm, with incremental clarity on biosimilar impacts and mitigation strategies.
  • Analysts maintained a focus on STELARA erosion and MedTech growth, with added emphasis on tariffs and margin pressures.
  • Management’s confidence in TREMFYA and new MedTech products remained a consistent theme.

Risks and Concerns

  • Biosimilar competition for STELARA remains a significant revenue headwind.
  • Analysts highlighted potential tariff-related cost pressures and their impact on MedTech margins.
  • Management acknowledged competitive dynamics in MedTech, particularly in electrophysiology and orthopedics, alongside challenges in the Chinese market.
  • Mitigation strategies include portfolio diversification, targeted investments in growth areas like TREMFYA and cardiovascular technologies, and operational efficiency improvements.

Final Takeaway

Johnson & Johnson demonstrated resilience in Q1 2025, overcoming significant headwinds from STELARA's loss of exclusivity. The company’s innovation-driven portfolio, especially in oncology and immunology, alongside robust MedTech growth, supports a confident outlook for 2025. Management's emphasis on operational efficiency and targeted investment further underscores their commitment to long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
